threat.title: Kubernetes RBAC Privilege Escalation via Misconfigured Role Bindings
threat.description: Overly permissive Kubernetes RBAC configurations allow attackers with minimal pod creation permissions to escalate privileges to cluster-admin level. Common misconfigurations include wildcard resource permissions, abuse of 'bind', 'escalate', and 'impersonate' verbs, unrestricted service account token mounting, and cross-namespace privilege escalation through role binding manipulation. This enables container escape, credential theft, and complete cluster takeover.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker gains initial access with limited pod creation permission in namespace 'dev'
2. Creates malicious pod with hostPath mount to access host filesystem /var/lib/kubelet
3. Extracts service account tokens from mounted volume secrets
4. Uses 'impersonate' verb to assume cluster-admin identity via privileged service account
5. Creates new ClusterRoleBinding granting cluster-admin to attacker's service account
6. Achieves full cluster control: deploys cryptominers, exfiltrates secrets, creates backdoor pods
7. Persists access through additional service accounts and webhook configurations
prerequisites: Access to Kubernetes cluster with valid service account token
RBAC misconfiguration: wildcard permissions ('*') on resources or verbs
Default service account auto-mount enabled (automountServiceAccountToken: true)
No PodSecurityPolicy or admission controller enforcement
Cross-namespace role binding allowed without restrictions

cve_references.description: Kubernetes privilege escalation via API server proxy request handling flaw allowing unauthorized backend access
API server allows unauthorized namespace access via volume subpath, enabling privilege escalation across namespaces
Denial of Service via YAML bomb in kubectl allowing resource exhaustion
Privilege escalation from compromised node to cluster via apiserver proxy

real_world_breaches.impact: Cryptomining malware deployed on Tesla's AWS Kubernetes infrastructure; credentials exposed, compute resources hijacked
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unauthenticated Kubernetes dashboard exposed to internet with default RBAC allowing cluster-admin access

real_world_breaches.incident: Shopify Bug Bounty - Kubernetes RBAC Bypass
real_world_breaches.impact: $25,000 bounty; researcher achieved cluster-admin from limited service account through role binding manipulation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Overly permissive RBAC allowing 'bind' verb on ClusterRoles without restrictions

real_world_breaches.impact: Disclosed method to escape AKS node restrictions and access cluster secrets via RBAC misconfiguration
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Default service account with excessive permissions combined with hostPath volume access

real_world_breaches.impact: First malware targeting Windows containers and Kubernetes clusters; used RBAC exploitation for persistence
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Compromised containers with service account tokens escalated to cluster-admin via role binding abuse

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Kubernetes RBAC privilege escalation via ClusterRole binding abuse in Shopify infrastructure
Cross-namespace privilege escalation through impersonate verb exploitation

security_research.title: Kubernetes RBAC: Security Hardening Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: Over 60% of production Kubernetes clusters have RBAC misconfigurations enabling privilege escalation

security_research.title: Attacking and Defending Kubernetes: Breaking the Cluster
security_research.key_finding: Most common privilege escalation vector is through service account token abuse with overprivileged RBAC

security_research.title: Threat Matrix for Kubernetes - Microsoft Security
security_research.key_finding: Privilege escalation through RBAC misconfiguration is MITRE ATT&CK technique T1078.004

mitigations.title: Implement Least-Privilege RBAC Policies with Explicit Resource Names
mitigations.description: Replace wildcard permissions with explicit resource names and minimal required verbs. Deny dangerous verbs like 'bind', 'escalate', 'impersonate' to non-admin roles. Use namespaced Roles instead of ClusterRoles wherever possible.

code_example.code:
# BAD: Overly permissive role with wildcards
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   name: dangerous-admin
# rules:
# - apiGroups: ["*"]
#   resources: ["*"]
#   verbs: ["*"]

# GOOD: Least-privilege role with explicit resources
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: app-developer
  namespace: production
rules:
# Allow read-only access to pods (no exec, no logs with sensitive data)
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
  
# Allow pod creation but restrict to specific service account
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["create", "delete", "update", "patch"]
  resourceNames: []  # Empty means restricted by admission controller
  
# Allow deployment management for app updates
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "update", "patch"]
  
# Explicitly deny dangerous verbs (use OPA/Kyverno for enforcement)
# NO bind, escalate, impersonate verbs
# NO wildcard (*) on apiGroups, resources, or verbs
# NO access to secrets unless explicitly required

---
# Role binding to specific user/service account
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: app-developer-binding
  namespace: production
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: app-deployer-sa
  namespace: production
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: app-developer
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

---
# Audit RBAC for dangerous permissions
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: rbac-audit-script
data:
  audit.sh: |
    #!/bin/bash
    # Check for wildcard permissions
    kubectl get clusterroles -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.rules[].verbs[] == "*") | .metadata.name'
    kubectl get roles --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.rules[].verbs[] == "*") | .metadata.name'
    
    # Check for dangerous verbs
    echo "Roles with 'bind' verb:"
    kubectl get clusterroles -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.rules[].verbs[] == "bind") | .metadata.name'
    
    echo "Roles with 'escalate' verb:"
    kubectl get clusterroles -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.rules[].verbs[] == "escalate") | .metadata.name'
    
    echo "Roles with 'impersonate' verb:"
    kubectl get clusterroles -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.rules[].verbs[] == "impersonate") | .metadata.name'

iso27001_controls: A.9.1.2 - Access to networks and network services
A.9.2.3 - Management of privileged access rights
validation_steps: Run 'kubectl get clusterroles -o yaml' and verify no wildcard (*) in verbs/resources
Check for bind/escalate/impersonate verbs: kubectl get clusterroles -o json | jq '.items[].rules[] | select(.verbs[] | contains("bind", "escalate", "impersonate"))'
Use kubectl-who-can to verify: 'kubectl who-can create clusterrolebindings'
Test with unprivileged service account attempting to create role bindings (should fail)
Enable audit logging and review for RBAC authorization failures

mitigations.title: Disable Service Account Token Auto-Mounting
mitigations.description: Prevent automatic mounting of service account tokens in pods unless explicitly required. This limits the attack surface by denying pods access to Kubernetes API credentials by default.

code_example.code:
# Method 1: Disable at ServiceAccount level (recommended)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: restricted-app-sa
  namespace: production
automountServiceAccountToken: false  # Prevent auto-mounting

---
# Method 2: Disable at Pod level
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: webapp-pod
  namespace: production
spec:
  serviceAccountName: restricted-app-sa
  automountServiceAccountToken: false  # Override even if SA allows it
  
  containers:
  - name: webapp
    image: myapp:v1.0.0
    # Application does not need Kubernetes API access
    # No /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ mounted

---
# Method 3: For pods that NEED API access, use projected token volumes (Kubernetes 1.20+)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: api-client-pod
  namespace: production
spec:
  serviceAccountName: api-client-sa
  automountServiceAccountToken: false  # Disable default mount
  
  containers:
  - name: api-client
    image: kubectl:v1.28.0
    volumeMounts:
    - name: kube-api-token
      mountPath: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount
      readOnly: true
  
  volumes:
  - name: kube-api-token
    projected:
      sources:
      - serviceAccountToken:
          path: token
          expirationSeconds: 3600  # 1 hour token expiry (instead of 1 year default)
          audience: https://kubernetes.default.svc  # Bind to specific audience
      - configMap:
          name: kube-root-ca.crt
          items:
          - key: ca.crt
            path: ca.crt
      - downwardAPI:
          items:
          - path: namespace
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace

---
# Admission controller policy (OPA Gatekeeper) to enforce no auto-mount
apiVersion: templates.gatekeeper.sh/v1
kind: ConstraintTemplate
metadata:
  name: k8sdisableautomounttokens
spec:
  crd:
    spec:
      names:
        kind: K8sDisableAutoMountTokens
  targets:
    - target: admission.k8s.gatekeeper.sh
      rego: |
        package k8sdisableautomounttokens
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          input.review.object.kind == "Pod"
          input.review.object.spec.automountServiceAccountToken == true
          msg := "Pod must not auto-mount service account tokens. Set automountServiceAccountToken: false"
        }
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          input.review.object.kind == "ServiceAccount"
          input.review.object.automountServiceAccountToken == true
          msg := "ServiceAccount must not enable auto-mount. Set automountServiceAccountToken: false"
        }

---
apiVersion: constraints.gatekeeper.sh/v1beta1
kind: K8sDisableAutoMountTokens
metadata:
  name: no-auto-mount-tokens
spec:
  match:
    kinds:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        kinds: ["Pod", "ServiceAccount"]
  parameters: {}

nist_csf: PR.PT-3 - The principle of least functionality is incorporated
validation_steps: Check existing pods: kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.automountServiceAccountToken != false) | .metadata.name'
Verify no /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount in pod: kubectl exec <pod> -- ls /var/run/secrets/
Deploy test pod with automountServiceAccountToken: false and confirm no token mount
Implement OPA Gatekeeper constraint and test with violating pod manifest (should be rejected)
Review service accounts: kubectl get sa --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.automountServiceAccountToken != false)'

mitigations.title: Enforce Pod Security Standards with Admission Controllers
mitigations.description: Implement Pod Security Admission (PSA) or third-party admission controllers (OPA, Kyverno) to prevent privileged pods, host namespace access, and dangerous volume mounts (hostPath, host IPC/PID).

code_example.code:
# Method 1: Use built-in Pod Security Standards (Kubernetes 1.25+)
# Apply to namespace level
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: production
  labels:
    # Enforce restricted security standard (most secure)
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version: latest
    
    # Audit violations (log but don't block)
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit: restricted
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit-version: latest
    
    # Warn users about violations
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn: restricted
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn-version: latest

---
# Method 2: OPA Gatekeeper Policy - Block privileged pods
apiVersion: templates.gatekeeper.sh/v1
kind: ConstraintTemplate
metadata:
  name: k8spspprivilegedcontainer
spec:
  crd:
    spec:
      names:
        kind: K8sPSPPrivilegedContainer
  targets:
    - target: admission.k8s.gatekeeper.sh
      rego: |
        package k8spspprivileged
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          container := input.review.object.spec.containers[_]
          container.securityContext.privileged == true
          msg := sprintf("Privileged container is not allowed: %v", [container.name])
        }
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          container := input.review.object.spec.initContainers[_]
          container.securityContext.privileged == true
          msg := sprintf("Privileged init container is not allowed: %v", [container.name])
        }

---
apiVersion: constraints.gatekeeper.sh/v1beta1
kind: K8sPSPPrivilegedContainer
metadata:
  name: no-privileged-containers
spec:
  match:
    kinds:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        kinds: ["Pod"]
    excludedNamespaces: ["kube-system", "gatekeeper-system"]

---
# Method 3: Kyverno Policy - Comprehensive pod security
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-pod-security
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Restricted)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/description: |
      Implements restricted Pod Security Standard:
      - No privileged containers
      - No hostPath, hostIPC, hostPID, hostNetwork
      - Required security context settings
      - No dangerous capabilities
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  background: true
  rules:
  
  # Rule 1: Block privileged containers
  - name: no-privileged-containers
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Privileged mode is not allowed. Set securityContext.privileged to false."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - =(securityContext):
              =(privileged): false
          =(initContainers):
          - =(securityContext):
              =(privileged): false
          containers:
          - =(securityContext):
              =(privileged): false
  
  # Rule 2: Block host namespace access
  - name: no-host-namespaces
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Host namespaces (hostPID, hostIPC, hostNetwork) are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(hostPID): false
          =(hostIPC): false
          =(hostNetwork): false
  
  # Rule 3: Block dangerous hostPath volumes
  - name: no-hostpath-volumes
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "HostPath volumes are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(volumes):
          - X(hostPath): "null"
  
  # Rule 4: Require non-root user
  - name: require-non-root-user
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Containers must run as non-root user. Set securityContext.runAsNonRoot to true."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - securityContext:
              runAsNonRoot: true
  
  # Rule 5: Drop dangerous capabilities
  - name: drop-all-capabilities
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "All capabilities must be dropped. Set securityContext.capabilities.drop to ['ALL']."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - securityContext:
              capabilities:
                drop:
                - ALL
  
  # Rule 6: Block privilege escalation
  - name: no-privilege-escalation
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Privilege escalation is not allowed. Set securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation to false."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - securityContext:
              allowPrivilegeEscalation: false

---
# Example: Secure pod template compliant with all policies
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: secure-webapp
  namespace: production
spec:
  serviceAccountName: webapp-sa
  automountServiceAccountToken: false
  
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    runAsUser: 10001
    fsGroup: 10001
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  
  containers:
  - name: webapp
    image: webapp:v1.0.0
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 10001
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    
    volumeMounts:
    - name: tmp
      mountPath: /tmp
    - name: cache
      mountPath: /var/cache
  
  volumes:
  - name: tmp
    emptyDir: {}
  - name: cache
    emptyDir: {}

nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created
validation_steps: Enable Pod Security Admission on cluster: kubectl label namespace production pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted
Test with privileged pod manifest (should be rejected): kubectl apply -f privileged-pod.yaml
Install Kyverno: kubectl create -f https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno/releases/download/v1.10.0/install.yaml
Apply ClusterPolicy and verify with: kubectl get cpol
Attempt to deploy pod with hostPath volume (should fail)
Review policy violations: kubectl get policyreport -A

mitigations.title: Implement RBAC Deny Policies for Dangerous Verbs
mitigations.description: Create explicit deny rules for dangerous RBAC verbs (bind, escalate, impersonate) and enforce through admission controllers. Monitor for any role/rolebinding creation attempts with these verbs.

code_example.code:
# Kyverno ClusterPolicy to block dangerous RBAC verbs
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-dangerous-rbac-verbs
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Dangerous RBAC Verbs
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Security
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: critical
    policies.kyverno.io/description: |
      Blocks creation of Roles/ClusterRoles with dangerous verbs:
      - bind: Allows binding roles to users/groups
      - escalate: Allows granting higher permissions than currently held
      - impersonate: Allows assuming another user's identity
      These verbs enable privilege escalation attacks.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  background: false
  rules:
  
  # Block 'bind' verb in ClusterRoles
  - name: block-bind-verb-clusterrole
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ClusterRole
    validate:
      message: "ClusterRole cannot contain 'bind' verb. This enables privilege escalation."
      deny:
        conditions:
          any:
          - key: "{{ request.object.rules[].verbs[] }}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value: ["bind", "*"]
  
  # Block 'escalate' verb in ClusterRoles
  - name: block-escalate-verb-clusterrole
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ClusterRole
    validate:
      message: "ClusterRole cannot contain 'escalate' verb. This enables privilege escalation."
      deny:
        conditions:
          any:
          - key: "{{ request.object.rules[].verbs[] }}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value: ["escalate", "*"]
  
  # Block 'impersonate' verb in ClusterRoles
  - name: block-impersonate-verb-clusterrole
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ClusterRole
    validate:
      message: "ClusterRole cannot contain 'impersonate' verb. This enables user impersonation attacks."
      deny:
        conditions:
          any:
          - key: "{{ request.object.rules[].verbs[] }}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value: ["impersonate", "*"]
  
  # Block wildcard verbs in ClusterRoles
  - name: block-wildcard-verbs-clusterrole
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ClusterRole
    validate:
      message: "ClusterRole cannot use wildcard (*) in verbs. Specify explicit verbs."
      deny:
        conditions:
          any:
          - key: "{{ request.object.rules[].verbs[] }}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value: ["*"]
  
  # Same rules for namespaced Roles
  - name: block-dangerous-verbs-role
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Role
    validate:
      message: "Role cannot contain dangerous verbs: bind, escalate, impersonate, or wildcard (*)"
      deny:
        conditions:
          any:
          - key: "{{ request.object.rules[].verbs[] }}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value: ["bind", "escalate", "impersonate", "*"]
  
  # Block wildcard resources in ClusterRoles
  - name: block-wildcard-resources
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ClusterRole
          - Role
    validate:
      message: "Cannot use wildcard (*) in resources. Specify explicit resource names."
      deny:
        conditions:
          any:
          - key: "{{ request.object.rules[].resources[] }}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value: ["*"]
  
  # Block ClusterRoleBindings to default service accounts
  - name: block-clusterrolebinding-to-default-sa
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ClusterRoleBinding
    validate:
      message: "Cannot bind ClusterRole to 'default' service account in any namespace."
      deny:
        conditions:
          any:
          - key: "{{ request.object.subjects[?kind=='ServiceAccount'].name }}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value: ["default"]

---
# OPA Gatekeeper equivalent policy
apiVersion: templates.gatekeeper.sh/v1
kind: ConstraintTemplate
metadata:
  name: k8sblockdangerousrbac
spec:
  crd:
    spec:
      names:
        kind: K8sBlockDangerousRBAC
      validation:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            dangerous_verbs:
              type: array
              items:
                type: string
  targets:
    - target: admission.k8s.gatekeeper.sh
      rego: |
        package k8sblockdangerousrbac
        
        dangerous_verbs := ["bind", "escalate", "impersonate", "*"]
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          input.review.object.kind == "ClusterRole"
          rule := input.review.object.rules[_]
          verb := rule.verbs[_]
          verb == dangerous_verbs[_]
          msg := sprintf("ClusterRole contains dangerous verb '%v' which enables privilege escalation", [verb])
        }
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          input.review.object.kind == "Role"
          rule := input.review.object.rules[_]
          verb := rule.verbs[_]
          verb == dangerous_verbs[_]
          msg := sprintf("Role contains dangerous verb '%v' which enables privilege escalation", [verb])
        }
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          input.review.object.kind == "ClusterRole"
          rule := input.review.object.rules[_]
          resource := rule.resources[_]
          resource == "*"
          msg := "ClusterRole cannot use wildcard (*) in resources"
        }
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          input.review.object.kind == "ClusterRoleBinding"
          subject := input.review.object.subjects[_]
          subject.kind == "ServiceAccount"
          subject.name == "default"
          msg := sprintf("Cannot bind ClusterRole to default service account in namespace %v", [subject.namespace])
        }

---
apiVersion: constraints.gatekeeper.sh/v1beta1
kind: K8sBlockDangerousRBAC
metadata:
  name: block-dangerous-rbac-verbs
spec:
  match:
    kinds:
      - apiGroups: ["rbac.authorization.k8s.io"]
        kinds: ["Role", "ClusterRole", "RoleBinding", "ClusterRoleBinding"]
  parameters:
    dangerous_verbs: ["bind", "escalate", "impersonate", "*"]

iso27001_controls: A.9.2.3 - Management of privileged access rights
nist_csf: PR.AC-4 - Access permissions and authorizations are managed
validation_steps: Install Kyverno: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno/releases/download/v1.10.0/install.yaml
Apply the ClusterPolicy: kubectl apply -f restrict-dangerous-rbac.yaml
Test by creating ClusterRole with 'bind' verb (should be rejected)
Audit existing roles: kubectl get clusterroles -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.rules[].verbs[] | contains("bind", "escalate", "impersonate"))'
Review policy reports: kubectl get policyreport -A -o wide
Monitor audit logs for RBAC violations in Azure Monitor or CloudWatch

indicators: Unexpected ClusterRoleBinding or RoleBinding creation events
Service account token requests from unusual namespaces or pods
API server requests with 'impersonate' headers (user, group, or extra scopes)
Audit log entries showing 'bind' or 'escalate' verb usage
New pods created with privileged security context or hostPath volumes
Cross-namespace resource access attempts (e.g., secrets from different namespace)
Sudden increase in API server requests from a single service account
kubectl exec or kubectl cp commands from unexpected source IPs
Creation of pods with serviceAccountName referencing high-privilege accounts
Modification of existing ClusterRoles or Roles adding dangerous permissions

log_queries.query:
// Detect RBAC privilege escalation attempts
AzureDiagnostics
| where Category == "kube-audit"
| where log_s contains "rbac.authorization.k8s.io"
| extend EventData = parse_json(log_s)
| extend Verb = tostring(EventData.verb)
| extend User = tostring(EventData.user.username)
| extend Resource = tostring(EventData.objectRef.resource)
| extend Name = tostring(EventData.objectRef.name)
| extend ResponseCode = tostring(EventData.responseStatus.code)
// Detect dangerous RBAC operations
| where Resource in ("clusterroles", "clusterrolebindings", "roles", "rolebindings")
  and Verb in ("create", "update", "patch", "bind", "escalate", "impersonate")
  and ResponseCode == "201" or ResponseCode == "200"
| summarize Count = count() by User, Verb, Resource, Name, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Count > 3
| order by Count desc

// Kubernetes RBAC privilege escalation detection
event.dataset: "kubernetes.audit" AND 
  kubernetes.audit.objectRef.resource: ("clusterroles" OR "clusterrolebindings" OR "roles" OR "rolebindings") AND
  kubernetes.audit.verb: ("create" OR "update" OR "patch" OR "bind" OR "escalate" OR "impersonate") AND
  kubernetes.audit.responseStatus.code: (200 OR 201) AND
  NOT kubernetes.audit.user.username: "system:kube-controller-manager"

index=kubernetes sourcetype=kube:apiserver:audit
| spath input=_raw
| search objectRef.resource IN ("clusterroles", "clusterrolebindings", "roles", "rolebindings")
  verb IN ("create", "update", "patch", "bind", "escalate", "impersonate")
  responseStatus.code IN (200, 201)
| eval user=coalesce(user.username, "unknown")
| stats count by user, verb, objectRef.resource, objectRef.name, _time
| where count > 5
| sort -count

# EKS Cluster Audit Logs - RBAC Escalation
fields @timestamp, objectRef.resource, verb, user.username, objectRef.name, responseStatus.code
| filter objectRef.resource in ["clusterroles", "clusterrolebindings", "roles", "rolebindings"]
| filter verb in ["create", "update", "patch", "bind", "escalate", "impersonate"]
| filter responseStatus.code in [200, 201]
| filter user.username != "system:kube-controller-manager"
| stats count(*) by user.username, verb, objectRef.resource
| sort count desc

tools.name: Falco
tools.usage: Detects RBAC privilege escalation via custom rules for dangerous kubectl commands and API calls

tools.name: KubeLinter
tools.usage: Scans YAML manifests for RBAC misconfigurations before deployment: kube-linter lint deployment.yaml

tools.name: rbac-police
tools.usage: Identifies overly permissive RBAC policies in running clusters

tools.name: kube-bench
tools.usage: Audits cluster against CIS Kubernetes Benchmark including RBAC controls

tools.name: KubiScan
tools.usage: python3 kubiscan.py -rs to find risky subjects with privilege escalation potential

compliance: PCI-DSS 4.0 (Requirement 7.1 - Limit access to system components)
